candidates.name: Tina Kotek
candidates.notes: Governor since 2023. First openly lesbian governor elected in US history. Former Oregon House Speaker (longest-serving). Progressive Democrat. Supports strict gun control (Oregon Measure 114). Pro-choice. Supports sanctuary state policies. Opposes school vouchers. Focus on homelessness and housing affordability.

profile.education: University of Oregon (BS, Religious Studies); University of Washington (MA, International Studies & Comparative Religion)
profile.background: Policy advocate and legislator; served as Speaker of the Oregon House (2013-2022); became 39th governor in 2023 and one of first openly lesbian governors in U.S.

candidates.name: Ron Wyden
candidates.notes: Senator since 1996. Ranking member of Senate Finance Committee. Heritage Action 0% current, 5% lifetime. NRA F rated. Son of German Jewish refugees who fled Nazis. Pro-choice. Supports gun control. Supports DREAM Act. Strong privacy and digital rights advocate. Supports legalized marijuana. Oregon's longest-serving senator.

profile.education: Stanford University (BA Political Science), University of Oregon School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former director of Oregon Legal Services for the Elderly. Former U.S. House member for 15 years. In Senate since 1996 via special election. Finance Committee ranking member. Privacy and tech policy champion.

district_offices.address: 131 NW. Hawthorne Ave., Suite 107, The Jamison Building
105 Fir St., Suite 201, SAC Annex Building

candidates.name: Jeff Merkley
candidates.notes: Senator since 2009. Heritage Action 0% current, 9% lifetime. NRA F rated. Former Oregon House Speaker. Pro-choice. Supports assault weapons ban. Supports DREAM Act and pathway to citizenship. Strong climate advocate. Wife Mary was a Lutheran Volunteer Corps worker. Progressive caucus ally.

profile.education: Stanford University (BA International Relations), Princeton University Woodrow Wilson School (MPA)
profile.background: Former Oregon House Speaker and Habitat for Humanity executive. Presidential management fellow at Pentagon and CBO. World Affairs Council director. In Senate since 2009.
